preview_text:
The model favours Belgium to win at 38%, but edges into closer territory than sportsbook consensus suggests. Iran's win probability of 30% sits only 8 percentage points below Belgium's in the forecast, making this a notably competitive fixture by the model's assessment. The underdog threat is material.

The draw at 32% represents the modal outcome in the model's distribution, indicating genuine uncertainty about the match result. The three outcomes cluster within a 8-point band—no outcome falls below 30%—suggesting the match could plausibly unfold in any direction. This reflects a tight competitive balance rather than a heavily tilted contest.

The model and market diverge substantially. Sportsbooks compress Belgium's win to 67%, assign the draw just 21%, and reduce Iran's chance to 13%. That represents roughly a 28-point gap in Belgium's favour between the two sources and a 19-point gap on the draw. The Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard makes no claim as to which view is correct; the disagreement itself flags material uncertainty in how this fixture will resolve.
